title: acrostichum wilkesianum
intro: Acrostichum wilkesianum, also known as the Tahitian fern, is a species of fern that is native to the tropical islands of the Pacific Ocean. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Polynesian medicine, where it is used to treat a range of ailments including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Acrostichum wilkesianum include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tea or infused in hot water, and it is also used in traditional Polynesian remedies such as creams and salves.
